recalls.Description:
iHerb is recalling three varieties of California Gold Nutrition dietary supplements: Daily Prenatal Multi, Ultamins Women's Multivitamin, and Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin. The Daily Prenatal Multi comes in a white bottle with a white lid and a gold label, containing 60 fish gelatin softgel capsules. The Ultamins Women's Multivitamin and Women's 50+ Multivitamin are packaged in dark purple containers with gold labels, each holding 60 capsules in foil packaging. Specific batch numbers and expiration dates can be found on the back of the packaging. 

The recalled products include batch codes and expiration dates as follows: Daily Prenatal Multi (2307050A, 2404096A, 2411100A) expiring in 08/2025, 05/2026, and 11/2026; Ultamins Women's Multivitamin (V0532, V0533) expiring in 11/2026 and 07/2026; Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin (V0534, V0536) expiring in 07/2026 and 11/2026.
recalls.Title: iHerb Issues Recall for California Gold Nutrition Iron Supplements Due to Child Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iHerb toll-free at 888-430-4770, email ProductRecall@iherb.com, or visit https://www.iherb.com/help/contact or https://information.iherb.com/hc/en-us/articles/37894456653972-iHerb-Recalls-California-Gold-Nutrition-Iron-Supplements-Due-to-Risk-of-Child-Poisoning.

Products.Name: California Gold Nutrition Daily Prenatal Multi

Images.Caption: Label for Recalled Daily Prenatal Multi
Label for recalled Ultamins Women's Multivitamin package
Label for recalled Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin package

Manufacturers.Name: iHerb LLC, dba Madre Labs LLC, of Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, and other retailers from January 2019 to April 2025, priced between $8 and $18.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging for iron-containing supplements

Remedies.Name: Secure the supplements out of children's reach and contact iHerb for a refund and disposal instructions

recalls.metaDescription: iHerb recalls California Gold Nutrition Iron Supplements due to child poisoning risk. Learn how to secure and return the products safely.

recalls.Description: Kramer Laboratories is recalling Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu blister packs due to a significant risk of child poisoning. The affected product is a 24-count caplet pack labeled with "Safetussin," "Multi-Symptom," and "Cough, Cold & Flu," and is designed for adults with high blood pressure and diabetes. The packaging, featuring a blue, orange, and red cardboard box, does not meet the required child-resistant standards, allowing young children to access the tablets easily.
recalls.Title: Kramer Laboratories Issues Recall for Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu Blister Packs Due to Child Safety Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to Kramer Laboratories at 800-824-4894, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at kramerlabs@emersongroup.com. Additional information can be found at https://safetussin.com/recall.

Products.Name: Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu Blister Packs

Images.Caption: Recalled Safetussin Max Strength Multi-Symptom Cough, Cold and Flu, 24-count caplets

Retailers.Name: Available at HEB, Harris-Teeter, and various regional grocery stores and independent pharmacies across the country from July 2024 to March 2025 for approximately $11.

Distributors.Name: Kramer Laboratories, of Bridgewater, New Jersey (a subsidiary of Arcadia Consumer Healthcare, of Bridgewater, New Jersey)

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging poses a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Secure the product away from children and contact Kramer Laboratories for instructions on returning or disposing of the product for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Kramer Laboratories recalls Safetussin Max Strength Cough, Cold and Flu packs due to child safety risks. Learn more about the recall.
